name: Running along the Nansa River river trail
name_original: Running por la senda fluvial del río Nansa
region_intro: Jogging around Val De San Vicente offers a diverse landscape for runners, characterized by its coastal environment and the Oyambre Natural Park. The region features a blend of estuaries, marshes, extensive sandy beaches, and coastal paths, providing varied running surfaces. Inland, green hills and small valleys offer routes with varying elevations, catering to different fitness levels. This municipality on Cantabria's western coast provides a scenic backdrop for outdoor activities. Best jogging routes around Val De San Vicente The most popular jogging route is Running along the Nansa River river trail, a 5.7 miles (9.2 km) trail that takes 58 minutes to complete. This route follows the Nansa River, offering flat, accessible paths alongside the water. Another top favourite among local runners is Access to Pechón – Viewpoint of Sable Beach loop from Pechón, a moderate 7.4 miles (11.9 km) path. This route provides coastal views and opportunities to run near Sable Beach. Local runners also love the San Vicente de la Barquera loop from Parque Natural de Oyambre, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through Oyambre Natural Park, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Jogging around Val De San Vicente is defined by its coastal paths, estuaries, and green hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, suitable for different ability levels. The routes in Val De San Vicente are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 300 runners have used komoot to explore Val De San Vicente's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Val de San Vicente?
A: Val de San Vicente offers a diverse range of running terrains. You can expect routes that combine extensive sandy beaches, coastal paths along cliffs, flat paths alongside estuaries and marshes, and green hills with varying elevations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e999033419/running-along-the-nansa-river-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running along the Nansa River river trail&lt;/a&gt;, for example, features accessible paths along the water, while routes within &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/comillas/attractions/oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; often blend beaches, dunes, and coastal forests.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or those looking for an easy jog?
A: Yes, Val de San Vicente offers routes suitable for various fitness levels. While many routes are moderate, there are options for easier jogs, particularly along the flat paths of the estuaries and beaches. The region's diverse landscape ensures you can find a comfortable pace and distance.

Q: Can I find family-friendly jogging trails in Val de San Vicente?
A: Absolutely. The flat, accessible paths along the estuaries and beaches, such as those found within &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/comillas/attractions/oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, are often ideal for families. These areas provide safe and scenic environments for a leisurely jog with children.

Q: Are dogs allowed on the running trails in Val de San Vicente?
A: Many outdoor areas in Val de San Vicente, including some beaches and natural park trails, are generally dog-friendly. However, it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash and be mindful of local regulations, especially in protected areas like &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/comillas/attractions/oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, to protect wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for everyone.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in the area?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can jog past stunning beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682669" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuentes Beach&lt;/a&gt;, explore the diverse ecosystems of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5406017" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, or run alongside the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1507066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Rabia Estuary&lt;/a&gt;. The charming fishing village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2287761" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de la Maza&lt;/a&gt; are also prominent sights that many routes pass by or originate from.

Q: Are there any circular running routes available?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Val de San Vicente are designed as loops, offering a convenient way to explore the area without retracing your steps. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31903879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Access to Pechón – Viewpoint of Sable Beach loop from Pechón&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38186833" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera loop from Parque Natural de Oyambre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year to go jogging in Val de San Vicente?
A: The western coast of Cantabria generally offers pleasant conditions for outdoor activities. Spring and autumn provide mild temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for jogging. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal runs, though it can be warmer. Winter offers a quieter experience, but some paths might be wetter.

Q: How many running routes are available in Val de San Vicente?
A: There are over 20 dedicated running routes around Val de San Vicente, catering to various distances and difficulty levels. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Val de San Vicente?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars from over 10 reviews. Runners often praise the stunning coastal views, the diverse natural landscapes of &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/comillas/attractions/oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt;, and the variety of terrains, from sandy beaches to green hills.

Q: Are there options for public transport to access the running trails?
A: While specific public transport links directly to every trailhead might be limited, the central fishing village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/333494" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Vicente de la Barquera&lt;/a&gt; serves as a hub for many outdoor activities and is generally accessible by public transport. From there, many routes are within easy reach.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running routes?
A: Parking is generally available in and around the main towns and villages, such as San Vicente de la Barquera and Pechón, which often serve as starting points for many routes. For routes within &lt;a href="https://www.visitacity.com/en/comillas/attractions/oyambre-natural-park" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oyambre Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; or near popular beaches, designated parking areas are usually provided.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of running routes in Val de San Vicente?
A: The routes in Val de San Vicente offer a good mix of difficulty levels. Out of the 20 available routes, 2 are considered easy, 16 are moderate, and 2 are more difficult. This variety ensures that both casual joggers and more experienced runners can find suitable challenges.
tips: Wonderful and picturesque itinerary that runs along the Nansa riverbed and that you can start in the town of Pesués until you reach Camijanes. It is worth doing it in the spring-summer months since you can take advantage of taking a bath in one of the beaches along the route.
